evals.prompt: Add a /profile route to this Flask app that shows the logged-in user's name, email, and join date. Redirect to /login if not authenticated. The app uses session['user_id'], raw sqlite3, and a users table with id, name, email, created_at columns. Project is at C:\Users\RishikeshKumar\Documents\Study\AI\claude\campusX\expense-tracker\expense-tracker.
evals.expected_output: app.py /profile route added with auth guard, parameterised DB query (no password_hash), Python-side date formatting, and render_template call. profile.html created with card layout using CSS variable tokens only.
assertions: The /profile route redirects to login when session.get('user_id') is falsy
The SQL SELECT does not include password_hash
The SQL query uses a ? placeholder (not string formatting)
The date is formatted with strftime in the Python route, not in the Jinja template
profile.html is created and extends base.html
profile.html displays name, email, and the formatted date using {{ }} syntax
No hardcoded hex colour values appear in profile.html or the new CSS rules

evals.prompt: Update the shared base.html navbar to be session-aware. When session['user_id'] exists, show 'Hi, <name>' and a Logout link. When there's no session, show the existing Sign in and Get started links. Project is at C:\Users\RishikeshKumar\Documents\Study\AI\claude\campusX\expense-tracker\expense-tracker.
evals.expected_output: base.html navbar updated with a Jinja {% if session.get('user_id') %} block. Logged-in branch shows user name and logout link; guest branch shows sign in and register links. Brand logo remains outside the conditional.
assertions: base.html contains a {% if session.get('user_id') %} conditional in the navbar
The logged-in branch displays the user's name from the session
The logged-in branch contains a link to url_for('logout')
The guest branch contains links to login and register
The brand logo/name link is outside the conditional block

evals.prompt: Implement the full authenticated profile dashboard for this Flask app: update /profile in app.py with auth guard and DB query, create templates/profile.html, update the navbar in base.html to be session-aware, and add the needed CSS to style.css. Project is at C:\Users\RishikeshKumar\Documents\Study\AI\claude\campusX\expense-tracker\expense-tracker.
evals.expected_output: All four files correctly updated/created. Route guards access, fetches user data safely, formats date in Python. Profile card template uses only CSS variable tokens. Navbar conditional works for both logged-in and guest states.
assertions: app.py /profile route has an auth guard that redirects unauthenticated users
app.py /profile SQL query does not select password_hash
app.py /profile passes a pre-formatted date string (not a raw datetime) to the template
templates/profile.html is created and shows at least name, email, and join date
templates/base.html navbar has a session-aware if/else block
static/css/style.css has new rules for .profile-card and .profile-section
All new CSS colour values use var(--…) tokens, not hardcoded hex
The db connection is closed after fetching the user record
